focus: Define verification strategy for highest-leverage deep-review improvements
summary: Verification should prove depth at four layers: post-dispatch schema enforcement, prompt-pack contract visibility, reducer/dashboard persistence, graph candidate-coverage stop blockers, and manual sandbox scenarios with seeded bugs. Existing tests cover artifact presence, basic JSONL shape, graph dimension blockers, and blocked-stop dashboarding; new tests must require candidate/search-ledger rows, target selection, null-search evidence, and candidate-aware convergence.

label: Post-dispatch validation is the first proof gate
recommendation: Extend post-dispatch validation tests to reject bad findingsNew shape, missing candidate/search-ledger rows, uncited candidate evidence, unsupported dispositions, and shallow findingDetails.

label: Schema fixtures should include valid and invalid review-depth records
recommendation: Add fixtures under mcp_server/tests/fixtures/deep-loop for valid ledger, missing ledger, bad findingsNew array, uncited candidate row, and rich finding linked to candidate.

label: Prompt-pack tests must pin the candidate-ledger contract
recommendation: Assert rendered deep-review prompts contain candidateMatrix/searchLedger, targetSelection, dispositions, bugClass, invariant, producer, consumer, negativeTestSearched, evidenceRefs, and linkedFindingId.

label: Reducer needs a candidate-ledger fixture instead of another blocked-stop-only fixture
recommendation: Add candidate-ledger-session fixture and extend reducer-schema tests to assert candidateCoverage, searchDebt, ruledOutCandidates, and dashboard Search Ledger output.

label: Coverage graph tests should prove new review graph vocabulary
recommendation: Add graph tests for BUG_CLASS, INVARIANT, PRODUCER, CONSUMER, and TEST review nodes plus relations that support candidate coverage.

label: Candidate-aware STOP blockers must be additive to existing graph blockers
recommendation: Keep unresolved P0 and uncovered dimension tests green while adding STOP_BLOCKED cases for all dimensions covered but missing candidate-class or invariant/test coverage.

label: Manual playbooks need depth scenarios, not artifact-only scenarios
recommendation: Update DRV-009, DRV-015, DRV-032 and add DRV-016, DRV-024, DRV-030, and CP-058 for candidate-ledger and search-coverage proof.

label: Command-flow stress testing should use seeded bug targets
recommendation: Add CP-058 sandbox with producer/consumer mismatch and missing negative-test boundary; pass only when candidates are emitted, linked or ruled out, and searchCoverage prevents shallow PASS.

label: Success metrics must combine contract and behavior proof
recommendation: Measure invalid fixture rejection, valid fixture acceptance, existing regression preservation, structured clean-dimension null evidence, report searchCoverage, graphless fallback ledgers, and seeded bug-class detection.

summary: Focused test commands should include mcp_server post-dispatch, prompt-pack, coverage graph, and graph-aware-stop tests, plus system-spec-kit script tests for deep-review reducer schema and contract parity.

Minimum review-depth schema should include reviewDepthSchemaVersion, targetSelection, candidateMatrix/searchLedger, searchCoverage, linkedCandidateIds on findings, and disposition enum values finding, ruled_out, deferred, blocked, not_applicable.
Manual scenarios should prove real execution with actual artifacts, extending the existing playbook rule that scenarios inspect real files, JSONL events, graphEvents, and review-report output.
Post-dispatch validation tests are the concrete proof layer for stricter schema validation.
Candidate-ledger reducer fixtures prove null-search evidence survives into reducer, dashboard, and synthesis-facing state.
Graph vocabulary and candidate-coverage blocker tests prove producer/consumer/invariant graph expansion is meaningful.
Seeded manual command-flow testing proves the candidate matrix changes review behavior, not only schema shape.

direction: Use only report snapshot tests to prove less surface-level review
reason: Rejected. Report tests can prove visibility, but they cannot prove candidate generation, post-dispatch enforcement, reducer persistence, or stop blocking without schema, reducer, and graph tests.

direction: Use only graph convergence tests as the proof strategy
reason: Rejected. Graph tests are valuable after new node semantics exist, but graphless runs still need text/JSON ledger fallback and post-dispatch validation.

direction: Treat existing DRV-009 and CP-053 artifact tests as sufficient
reason: Rejected. They prove durable artifacts and canonical JSONL labels, not target selection, candidate search, null-search evidence, or seeded bug discovery.
